title: cynoglossum officinale subsp. rotatum
intro: Cynoglossum officinale subsp. rotatum, also known as the Hound's Tongue or Rotatum Hound's Tongue, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). The medicinal properties of Cynoglossum officinale subsp. rotatum include its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its use in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat respiratory issues, skin problems, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to act as an expectorant and anti-inflammatory agent. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Cynoglossum officinale subsp. rotatum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat coughs, colds, and skin irritations.
